name: companion
title: Companion
description: Manage and safely install SKILL.md packages through delegated Agent Auth, including pinned public releases, publishing, updates, dependencies, secrets, local install tracking, and Companion self-update.
notes:
## What it does

The Companion skill gives your assistant one reliable workflow for local SKILL.md packages: connect through delegated Agent Auth, validate, publish, update, organize, install, audit, and self-update. It can also publish one explicitly selected version of an organization skill as a stable public release.

## Safety model

- Reads only skill folders you point it at and Companion's own local state under ~/.companion.
- Stores only the non-secret Agent Auth reference in ~/.companion/credentials.json; private keys live in 0600 files under ~/.companion/agent-auth.
- Requests workspace-scoped capabilities progressively and never silently falls back to a preserved PAT. Legacy PAT use requires COMPANION_AUTH_MODE=legacy-pat.
- Confirms publishing or archival changes before mutating workspace skills, and defaults public-release promotion to no after every publication.
- Verifies the exact public ZIP checksum, rejects unsafe archive entries, and never executes packaged scripts during a public install.
- Uses companion.json as the source of truth for package identity, version, env/secrets, dependencies, notes, commands, and changelog.

## Local tracking

Companion records installed workspace skills in ~/.companion/skills.lock.json under workspace-id keys so audits can compare local versions, checksums, and dependency snapshots against the workspace registry without storing credentials. Installs fan out into every tool the user works with (Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, …); each record's targets[] array tracks the per-tool and per-project install locations, with project-scope installs kept in a per-project <repo>/.companion/skills.lock.json. Public-link installs only install the selected root ZIP and do not create a Companion skill_installs row.

changes: Bundles every runtime dependency into the standalone Agent Auth client distributed with Companion.
Documents the explicit Agent Auth connect action and its device-approval behavior.
Preserves the fixed public Agent Auth origin across production reverse proxies without widening JWT audiences.
Makes delegated Agent Auth the default identity, with progressive workspace-scoped capabilities, schema-v3 credential references, and private 0600 key storage.
Adds explicit management of a pinned public release and requires a default-no promotion choice after every new organization-skill version.
Installs an authenticated public release from its exact verified ZIP without scripts, dependency closure, secret resolution, or a workspace install record.

Rejects symbolic links throughout the selected install destination, proves physical root containment before replacement, and reports current, legacy, and frontmatter-fallback prerequisites without resolving them.
Preserves older PAT credentials only behind the explicit COMPANION_AUTH_MODE=legacy-pat compatibility mode and never falls back silently.
Keeps delegated secret redemption off argv and stdout by passing plaintext only through an inherited private pipe into the mode-0600 projection workflow.
Documents per-user, per-workspace sidebar ordering for personal and organization folders.
Clarifies that category ordering is private UI state and never mutates the shared label hierarchy.
Documents the browser-session-only skill preference snapshot and keeps PAT workflows out of it.
Documents the optional portable skill icon in companion.json manifest v2.
Defines the curated icon catalog and requires icon preservation across normalization and republication.
Documents nullable icon metadata on skill list and detail responses while keeping older packages compatible.
Refreshes a file-backed Companion token automatically when it expired within the last 30 days.
Preserves the token's exact name and scopes, revokes the expired token atomically, and stores the one-time replacement through a private atomic credentials-file swap.
Requires a fresh Use prompt for environment-provided, revoked, departed-user, or more-than-30-days-expired credentials without printing any token.
Removes the retired skill star endpoint and response fields from the documented Companion API.
Updates public-preview metadata guidance to match the star-free response contract.
Keeps skill discovery centered on libraries, labels, search, dependencies, and install state.
Documents token-readable GET /skills/{slug} metadata for the official installer.
Requires skills:read for canonical skill detail while preserving personal-skill privacy and workspace isolation.
Restores automatic installs for already-distributed Companion 1.21.0 clients without changing install_skill.py.
Gives Companion PATs parity with signed-in users for secret vault, binding, and suggestion mutations inside their workspace.
Adds create_secret.py --skill so agents resolve the declared slot, create a write-only secret, and bind it without a browser.
Keeps plaintext out of arguments and output while preserving workspace, ownership, audience, skill-access, and stable-slot checks.
Retrieves personal and shared secret bindings through five-minute preflights and non-replayable sixty-second grants.
Commits skill packages with private atomic .env projections, restores interrupted swaps on the next secrets operation, and removes transient plaintext backups.
Requires the explicit --confirm-secrets consent flag before retrieval and rejects the legacy metadata-only confirmation flag.
Keeps package-only installs compatible with legacy credentials and requires a workspace-id credential refresh before any secret retrieval.
Creates personal or shared write-only secrets through a dedicated secrets:write helper without putting values in command arguments or output.
Adds rotation/binding/tombstone sync, explicit manual profiles, offline stale-copy reporting, and value-free local projection state.
Documents the managed SaaS Free and Pro skill entitlements and the 20-skill organization quota.
Explains structured entitlement 403 responses and directs signed-in Owners/Admins to Billing without using PATs.
Prevents agents from retrying or bypassing personal-skill, version-history, Share, restore, rename, and frozen-catalog gates.
Deletes transient install and self-update backup folders in all normal success and failure paths.
Restores the previous skill folder during a failed swap without retaining a backup copy afterward.
Blocks stale backup folders containing SKILL.md in the local skill guard so agents delete them instead of treating them as real skills.
Reads the workspace skill naming policy before naming or filing a brand-new skill.
Applies the policy to skill slugs, package names, and folder choices when the org defines one.
Documents the token-readable GET /orgs/current/skill-naming-policy endpoint and its { policy } response.
Warns when the same local SKILL.md name is visible from multiple scanned paths with identical or missing Companion ids.
Includes duplicate local path evidence so users can manually remove stale skill copies without automatic deletion.
Keeps true retarget conflicts blocking when one slug maps to multiple Companion ids.
Adds OpenCode as a multi-tool install target, detected via ~/.config/opencode and installed through the shared Agent Skills paths ~/.agents/skills and .agents/skills.
Updates Companion install prompts and the Companion skills UI so users can choose OpenCode and report OpenCode as the installing assistant.
Documents multi-tool install fan-out across Claude Code, Codex, and OpenCode.
Documents dependency graph responses with deduplicated transitive dependencies, via provenance, and depth.
Documents per-dependency current version, installed version, and install_status update metadata.
Notes that dependency update status matches the Skills list signal for recorded installs behind the current published dependency closure.
Documents the modifiers array returned by GET /skills rows so agents can distinguish the creator from members who published later versions.
Installs a requested skill's transitive dependencies automatically before installing the requested root skill.
Blocks the root install when any dependency is missing, archived, cycle-blocked, not openable, locally customized, untracked, or blocked on required secrets.
Adds --confirm-required-secrets so agents can record that required secrets for the root and dependency closure were confirmed before package downloads or lockfile writes.
Installs a skill into every tool the user works with (Claude Code, Codex, …) in one fan-out, not just the tool in use at the moment.
Adds an extensible tool registry (scripts/tools.json) and records the machine's tool set in ~/.companion/config.json after auto-detecting present tools and confirming with the user.
Tracks each install location per tool and scope through a lockfile targets[] array: user-scope in ~/.companion/skills.lock.json and project-scope in a per-project <repo>/.companion/skills.lock.json.
Keeps the workspace install report aggregate (a single POST /skills/{slug}/install across all tools and projects) and leaves locally customized targets untouched unless forced.
Documents the browser-native file preview endpoint for package files returned by GET /skills/{slug}/versions/{version}/files.
Documents the explicit POST /skills/{slug}/rename workflow for renaming a skill while preserving its stable Companion skill id.
Instructs agents to update local SKILL.md frontmatter and companion.json.name after rename while keeping metadata.companionSkillId unchanged.
Adds a fast local bootstrap health check that returns workspace, Companion, integrity, and skill update context in one pass.
Allows Companion self-update from the bootstrap only when tracked local files still match an official installed-version baseline.
Ships a local integrity baseline so future Companion updates can detect customized tracked files across versions.
Detects and preserves local customizations to bootstrap and Companion scripts instead of overwriting them automatically.
Documents the share-plan preview endpoint for personal skills.
Documents that sharing a personal skill to the org also migrates required owner-private dependencies and returns shared_dependencies.
Requires agents to share a Skill link in chat after successful skill uploads or updates, using public org preview links when available.
Deletes only the backup created by a successful Companion self-update after the install report succeeds, while preserving older backups and keeping the new backup if reporting fails.
Requires confirmation that declared required secrets are ready, or explicit user authorization to install without them, before installing or updating workspace-published skills.
Documents org-skill public preview links and the metadata-only GET /public/skills/{share_token} endpoint.
Notes the session-only share-target resolver used by the web app to open links in the right workspace.
Notes that skill list rows include share_token for building clean /s/{token} share URLs.
Adds a mandatory local preflight guard (scripts/skill_guard.py) to run before create, update, install, or lockfile writes, blocking duplicate slugs and skill-id retargets.
Refuses to create a skill whose slug already exists online or locally, and always sends expect_slug + expect_skill_id on updates now that the server requires them.
Automatically migrates and removes the legacy skills.log.json after copying entries into skills.lock.json, and flags archived or missing skills as missing_or_archived instead of current.
Adds a CI guard requiring companion.json.version to increase when bundled Companion skill files change.
Updates install confirmation examples so they report the current bundled Companion version.
Documents token-supported workspace skill listing, including My Skills and installed-only filters.
Adds a local Python update check that compares workspace listings, reported installs, and ~/.companion/skills.lock.json.
Declares local-only manifest checks so agents can run update audits without the Companion API executing skill scripts.
Updates Companion manifest examples to use the thecompanion.sh schema URL.
Keys ~/.companion/credentials.json and ~/.companion/skills.lock.json by Companion workspace id.
Keeps tokens only in credentials.json; the skills lockfile stores apiUrl metadata but never secrets.
Documents migration from URL-keyed lockfiles and the legacy skills.log.json alias.
Moves Companion registry identity and version out of SKILL.md metadata and into companion.json.
Adds manifest v2 with markdown notes, env/secrets declarations, dependency name-to-id mapping, commands, and changelog metadata.
Adds local install tracking guidance for ~/.companion/skills.lock.json.
Runs the mandatory Companion self-update check once per conversation instead of before every Companion turn.
Reports org skills published through Companion as installed for the current user.
Runs a mandatory Companion self-update check at the start of every Companion invocation before validating, publishing, updating, labeling, or installing any other skill.

Installs newer bundled Companion versions through /local-skills/companion/package, verifies the staged package, backs up the old folder, reports the installed version, and then asks the user to rerun when runtime reload is not guaranteed.
Requires explicit publish placement for brand-new skills: Personal/My Skills vs Org/everyone, then existing folder, new folder, or no folder before POST /skills.
Documents scope as valid on first publish only, with updates preserving existing scope and labels unless the user explicitly adds folders.

COMPANION_API_URL.description: Workspace API base URL. The install/use prompt stores this in ~/.companion/credentials.json when the environment variable is not set.

COMPANION_WORKSPACE_ID.description: Companion workspace id (organizations.id). The install/use prompt stores credentials and local install inventory under this workspace key.

COMPANION_TOKEN.description: Optional compatibility-only personal access token. It is ignored unless the user explicitly selects COMPANION_AUTH_MODE=legacy-pat; Agent Auth failures never fall back to it.

commands.name: Connect an agent
commands.desc: Discover the instance and approve delegated, progressively requested Agent Auth capabilities for one workspace.

commands.name: Use explicit legacy credentials
commands.desc: Use or refresh a preserved PAT only after the user explicitly selects legacy-pat mode.

commands.name: Bootstrap health check
commands.desc: Gather workspace, self-update, local integrity, and installed-skill context in one fast pass.

commands.name: List skills
commands.desc: List org, My Skills, reported installed skills, and local lockfile inventory.

commands.name: Publish a skill
commands.desc: Validate a skill, ask Personal vs Org and folder placement, then publish it safely.

commands.name: Update a skill
commands.desc: Push a new version with targeted identity checks.

commands.name: Manage a public release
commands.desc: Make the current version of an organization skill public, promote it explicitly, or remove package access while preserving the share link.

commands.name: Install a public release
commands.desc: Authenticate once, verify the exact pinned ZIP, inspect prerequisites, and atomically install only the safe root package.

commands.name: Rename a skill
commands.desc: Change a skill slug through the explicit rename API while preserving its stable id.

commands.name: Organize with labels
commands.desc: Create, assign, rename, recolor, or delete org and personal folder labels.

commands.name: Resolve dependencies
commands.desc: Analyze packages and sync companion.json before upload.

commands.name: Sync companion.json
commands.desc: Create or repair manifest v2 with identity, env/secrets, dependency ids, notes, commands, and changelog.

commands.name: Manage skill API calls
commands.desc: Use the supported skills API surface without crossing into workspace admin.

commands.name: Manage and retrieve secrets
commands.desc: Create and bind write-only personal or shared secrets without a browser, manage their lifecycle, then retrieve authorized values through one-time grants.

commands.name: Update Companion skill
commands.desc: Check and install the latest bundled Companion skill safely.

commands.name: Guard against duplicates
commands.desc: Preflight the local inventory and workspace catalog before create/update/install to block duplicate slugs and skill-id retargets.

commands.name: Check for updates
commands.desc: Compare installed skills and dependencies against the workspace registry.

commands.name: Install into your tools
commands.desc: Fan out a skill install into every configured tool (Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode) at user or project scope.

commands.name: Install updates
commands.desc: Bring outdated skills up to the latest published version.

commands.name: Validate a skill
commands.desc: Check a skill is well formed before you share it.

commands.name: Manage your skills
commands.desc: List and organize the skills on this machine.
